test_completeness.enumeration_basis: Every routing branch of attachTouchScroll/routeScroll changed by this diff, plus the user-facing device outcome.
test_completeness.justification: Client-only routing fix; each branch is unit-tested at the call site. The byte-level mouse-report encoding is xterm's own code and needs a live renderer (getMouseReportCoords requires valid char size) jsdom lacks, so the on-device outcome is iPad UAT.

behaviors.behavior: alt-screen buffer: a touch-pan dispatches a pixel-mode WheelEvent (deltaY = raw px delta, deltaMode 0, finger coords) on term.element and does NOT call scrollLines
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts: down-pan deltaY=+100, up-pan deltaY=-100, deltaMode===0, scrollLines not called, coords flow through (5 routing tests PASSED). Inverts the ADR-131/132 arrow-key assertions.

behaviors.behavior: normal buffer WITH mouse tracking (.enable-mouse-events): the wheel path is taken (app consumes the report), NOT scrollLines
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'normal buffer WITH mouse tracking' PASSED (wheel dispatched len 1, scrollLines not called).

behaviors.behavior: normal buffer, no mouse tracking: scrollback preserved via consumeTouchDelta -> term.scrollLines(lines), no wheel dispatched
behaviors.evidence: touch-scroll.alt-buffer.test.ts 'normal buffer, no mouse tracking' (scrollLines(5), 0 wheel) + touch-scroll.test.ts mock cohort 13 tests PASSED.

behaviors.behavior: defensive: term.element absent (pre-open) falls back to scrollLines without throwing
end-to-end on a real iPad: one-finger pan scrolls Claude's TUI (not input-history cycling) and matches the trackpad feel
behaviors.evidence: Needs a real touchscreen + live xterm renderer + Claude's negotiated mouse protocol; getMouseReportCoords returns undefined without a renderer in jsdom. iPad UAT is the post-deploy gate (per ADR-131/132 precedent).

surface_verification.justification: Real-device-only behavior. The fix changes only client touch->wheel routing (no Vite-config/CORS/WS-Origin/proxy touched, so the browser-smoke trigger does not apply). A desktop Playwright run cannot reproduce the bug: synthetic touch bypasses the gesture arbitration + there is no real Claude mouse-mode in an E2E, so it would be GREEN-but-meaningless (the exact 'tests green, device broken' trap ADR-131 warns about). The 4 routing branches are unit-tested; the on-device outcome is iPad UAT by the user.
